citation.quote: The game is played on a grid of nine cells, numbered 1 through 9. A mark may only ever be placed in one of these nine cells.
X moves first. Turns alternate strictly between X and O. On a turn, the moving player places exactly one mark of their own symbol.
A mark may be placed only in an empty cell. A cell containing any mark is occupied and cannot be chosen.
A player wins upon having three of their marks in any straight line: a row, a column, or a diagonal.
The game ends immediately when a player wins, or when all nine cells are occupied without a winner (a draw). After the game has ended, no further marks may be placed.
